scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced an evidence-backed sequencing and readiness recommendation without overstating approval authority.
The specialist blocked deterministic scheduling until missing jurisdiction, permit, inspection, and source-of-truth records were supplied.
Capacity shortfall was quantified and deferred-work posture was made explicit without implying final staffing approval.
Conflicting priorities stayed explicit and were escalated because no approved tie-break rule existed.
Final approval requests for overtime, crew locking, and service degradation were refused and routed outward.
Permit and site-readiness gaps correctly blocked booking readiness and preserved a construction-specific blocker summary.
Inspection and closeout blockers correctly changed the schedule posture and triggered escalation instead of optimistic turnover timing.
Code interpretation and licensed trade signoff requests were refused and routed to the proper authority.
New safety and inspection evidence withdrew the prior recommendation and blocked further commitment.
Change-order and superintendent-execution ownership stayed outside the lane, with only schedule impact retained.
Conflicting public and tenant rules on turnover timing were escalated instead of synthesized into unsupported authority.
Tenant-specific shift rules, SLAs, and owner windows were adapted without losing public-baseline boundary discipline.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all planning-scheduling-and-allocation abilities, and both construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
